frameworks.name: population coding
frameworks.one_line: brain function emerges from coordinated activity across many neurons

frameworks.name: deep neural network decoding
frameworks.one_line: modern ML approach for behavioral decoding from neural population activity

core_question: How do classic mathematical and modern machine-learning methods shape conclusions drawn from whole-brain neural recordings?
key_assumptions: brain functions emerge from coordinated activity of many neurons
useful information can be extracted from dense WBA recordings via dimensionality reduction
method choice constrains the inferences one can draw about brain function
manipulations: comparison of classic linear methods (PCA, ICA) versus deep learning decoders
methodological choices applied to whole-brain activity datasets
key_findings: classic dimensionality-reduction tools (PCA, ICA) extract interpretable structure from WBA data
deep neural networks enable behavioral decoding from densely-recorded population activity
different methodological choices entail different assumptions about neural computation
interpretations: understanding brain function requires combining classic mathematical tools with modern ML approaches
method assumptions directly shape the conclusions drawn about how the brain works
